articles.title: Pentagon releases initial batch of declassified files detailing UFOs
articles.summary: The Pentagon released a batch of “never‑before‑seen” declassified files on UFOs after President Donald Trump directed the agency to do so earlier in the year. The files contain investigations of reported sightings that span decades, including a large FBI file with hundreds of pages of eyewitness testimonies and public reports from 1947 to 1968 and more recent military memos describing a possible small UAP in Iraq in 2022 and multiple glares or lights of unknown origin observed in Syria in 2024.

en.title: Pentagon releases initial batch of declassified files detailing UFOs
en.summary: The Pentagon released a batch of “never‑before‑seen” declassified files on UFOs after President Donald Trump directed the agency to do so earlier in the year. The files contain investigations of reported sightings that span decades, including a large FBI file with hundreds of pages of eyewitness testimonies and public reports from 1947 to 1968 and more recent military memos describing a possible small UAP in Iraq in 2022 and multiple glares or lights of unknown origin observed in Syria in 2024.

de.title: Das Pentagon veröffentlicht den ersten Stapel freigegebener UFO-Dateien
de.summary: Das Pentagon veröffentlichte eine Reihe „nie zuvor veröffentlichter“ freigegebener UFO-Dateien, nachdem Präsident Donald Trump die Behörde Anfang des Jahres dazu angewiesen hatte. Die Akten enthalten Untersuchungen zu gemeldeten Sichtungen, die sich über Jahrzehnte erstrecken, darunter eine große FBI-Akte mit Hunderten von Seiten an Augenzeugenaussagen und öffentlichen Berichten von 1947 bis 1968 sowie neuere militärische Memos, die eine mögliche kleine UAP im Irak im Jahr 2022 und mehrere in Syrien im Jahr 2024 beobachtete Blendungen oder Lichter unbekannter Herkunft beschreiben.

articles.title: Pentagon UFO file trove includes sightings near Japan
articles.summary: The Japanese government’s top spokesman announced that Tokyo is studying a trove of previously classified Pentagon files on alleged UFO sightings. The files include two videos of unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAP) that were spotted near Japan. Chief Cabinet Secretary Minoru Kihara said at a regular news conference that Japan is constantly gathering and analyzing information on incidents related to national security, including UAPs.

articles.title: ‘SLEEPING DOG’ Trailer Dives Into Jeremy Corbell’s Fight For UAP Disclosure
articles.summary: The trailer for “SLEEPING DOG” offers a first look at a documentary that follows investigative journalist and filmmaker Jeremy Corbell and his controversial rise in the UAP disclosure movement. Directed by Michael Lazovsky, the film has been described as a real‑life “Disclosure Day” and charts Corbell’s transition from mixed martial artist to a prominent figure pushing for transparency around unidentified aerial phenomena. It will open in select theaters on May 8, 2026, before becoming available on digital platforms on May 12.

summaries.batch-declassified-detailing-files-initi: The Pentagon released a batch of “never‑before‑seen” declassified files on UFOs after President Donald Trump directed the agency to do so earlier in the year. The files contain investigations of reported sightings that span decades, including a large FBI file with hundreds of pages of eyewitness testimonies and public reports from 1947 to 1968 and more recent military memos describing a possible small UAP in Iraq in 2022 and multiple glares or lights of unknown origin observed in Syria in 2024. The Pentagon said the documents are now available to the public with no clearance required and will post new materials on a rolling basis every few weeks. A disclaimer on the Pentagon website notes that the descriptive language in the military memos reflects the subjective interpretation of the writer and should not be taken as a conclusive indication of what actually happened. The files also include reports from Apollo 12 astronauts in 1969 who saw flashes of light sailing off the Moon and from Apollo 17 astronauts in 1972 who saw bright particles of light that they theorized might be chunks of ice. Trump’s Truth Social post and NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman’s statement both praised the release as a step toward greater transparency about unidentified anomalous phenomena.
summaries.advisor-amid-bizarre-defence-disclosure-: Serhii “Flash” Beskrestnov, a senior adviser to Ukraine’s Minister of Defence, revealed on Telegram that the Ukrainian Armed Forces run a formal program to track and study unidentified flying objects, a directive that has been in place since the early days of the war with Russia. The program is sanctioned at the highest military level by the Commander‑in‑Chief and is framed as a national‑security necessity because unidentified aerial activity over Ukrainian territory could represent new Russian weapons technology. Beskrestnov claims that UFOs might be secret Russian weapons and posted 2023 reconnaissance footage as “proof.” He said he was approached by officials from a Ukrainian state institution that has quietly handled such investigations since the Soviet period, and that a formal UAF document governing the study of such phenomena is already operational. The adviser urges anyone who captures unidentified objects on camera—public or military—to submit footage to uap@gur.gov.ua, noting that firsthand visual evidence could have significant intelligence value. His remarks came as the U.S. Pentagon moves forward with a declassification of millions of UFO‑related records, and on May 8 2026 the Department of War released the first set of 162 UFO files, of which 158 are live on the portal.
summaries.encounters-having-pilots-still-whistlebl: Fox News reported on May 9, 2026 that a whistleblower claims pilots are still having encounters with unidentified aerial phenomena. The headline states that pilots are “still having” these encounters. The article falls under the UAP category and is part of a cluster of Fox News sources. No additional description is provided in the available metadata. The report identifies the whistleblower as the source of the claim.
summaries.begins-can-files-make-minds-own-pentagon: The Pentagon has begun releasing new files on UFOs, stating that the public can draw their own conclusions about unidentified anomalous phenomena. The effort is led by the White House, the director of national intelligence, the Energy Department, NASA, and the FBI. In a post on X, the Pentagon said that past administrations had sought to discredit or dissuade the public, while President Donald Trump is focused on maximum transparency and allowing people to form their own opinions. The Pentagon will release additional documents on a rolling basis, and Trump has been teasing the announcement since February, having previously released records on the assassinations of President John F. Kennedy, Senator Robert F. Kennedy, and Martin Luther King Jr. that revealed little beyond what was already known. Congress created an office in 2022 to declassify UFO material, and its 2024 report found hundreds of new UAP incidents but no evidence that the U.S. government had confirmed alien technology. A small group of Republicans, including Rep. Anna Paulina Luna and Rep. Tim Burchett, have urged further transparency, with Luna demanding 46 UAP videos identified by whistleblowers and Burchett noting that transparency will take time; experts caution that UAP videos are often misinterpreted by those unfamiliar with advanced military technology.
summaries.batch-can-decide-first-pentagon-public-r: Spectrum News lists a UAP-related report dated 2026-05-08 under the headline "Pentagon releases first batch of UFO records as Trump says public can 'decide for them­selves'". The headline is treated as the source claim, and the app does not add details that are not present in the feed metadata. The feed metadata connects the topic with PENTAGON, TRUMP. Related headlines in the same topic cluster mention: Trump's Department of War Releases First Batch of Alien, UFO Files. For deeper context, use the linked sources below the article card.
summaries.cseo-cyprus-declassified-disclosure-head: George Danos, head of the Cyprus Space Exploration Organisation, said the release of declassified UAP material marks a new phase of disclosure. He told the Cyprus News Agency that the coming period could be key to determining whether humanity is alone or visited by advanced civilizations. Danos noted that the United States has declassified and released files on unidentified anomalous phenomena as part of a transparency initiative, and that multiple declassified videos have already been downloaded by hundreds of millions of people. He said public interest has grown rapidly and officials are now more willing to discuss incidents openly, noting that pilots who previously did not report sightings now do so. Danos mentioned that some officials have suggested humanity may not be alone and that a total change in how the issue is handled is expected. He claimed that some released footage shows unusual flight dynamics that are unlikely to match known human technology.
summaries.briefed-claim-congregations-disclosure-i: Several religious leaders, including Pastor Perry Stone and evangelist Tony Merkel, claim they were privately briefed by U.S. military intelligence officers to prepare their congregations for an upcoming UAP disclosure. Stone, a Tennessee‑based Pentecostal evangelist, said in a video that a trusted associate informed him that a group of pastors was invited to hear directly from government‑connected individuals about an imminent disclosure event involving unidentified anomalous phenomena. Stone described the briefing as covering craft not allegedly part of our planet, non‑human beings, and a coordinated propaganda plan already in motion. Merkel says he has been in contact with officials for over a year, describing them as Christians in intelligence operations assigned to work within the UAP disclosure community, and that they approached him with a mission to prepare the body of Christ for what is coming. The state where the briefing took place has not been disclosed, and no government memo, official invitation, transcript, or attendee list has surfaced. The White House has not denied the claims, and Washington has remained silent, a silence the article notes is significant.
summaries.administration-america-file-gov-historic: On May 8 2026 the Department of War announced the first release of new, never‑before‑seen government files on Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena (UAP), also known as UFOs. The release is part of the Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters (PURSUE), an interagency effort that includes the White House, the Office of the Director of National Intelligence, the Department of Energy, AARO, NASA, the FBI, and other intelligence components. The files are housed at WAR.GOV/UFO and more releases are expected on a rolling basis. The initial tranche contains more than 160 documents, ranging from State Department and FBI reports to NASA mission transcripts, eyewitness accounts, photographs, and a 1947 Pan Am crew sighting of a bright orange object. The Department of War notes that the cases are unresolved, meaning the government has not made a definitive determination about the phenomena, and that unresolved status may result from limited data, incomplete information, or the need for additional technical analysis. The files do not constitute official confirmation of alien technology, recovered alien materials, or extraterrestrial life, and Secretary of War Pete Hegseth described the release as a transparency effort aligned with President Trump’s order.
summaries.1972-2026-declassified-images-landing-mo: The Pentagon has released 17 newly declassified images of unidentified aerial phenomena. The images cover a period from the 1972 Apollo 11 moon landing to 2026. The release was reported by MSN on May 12, 2026. The article notes that the images were declassified by the Pentagon.
summaries.files-here-inside-its-just-pentagon-rele: Time Magazine published an article on May 11, 2026 titled “The Pentagon Just Released Its UFO Files. Here’s What’s Inside.” The piece reports that the Pentagon has released a set of previously classified UFO files. According to the article, the release includes documents that the Pentagon has made public. The article outlines what is contained within those files.
summaries.340-first-hits-hours-million-pentagon-re: Pentagon officials announced on Friday that the newly launched website http://WAR.GOV/UFO, created to release government UFO files, had received 340 million hits within its first 12 hours. Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell posted the figure on X, noting that the traffic came from Americans and “truth seekers worldwide” seeking unfiltered UAP information. He said the response demonstrates the Trump administration’s commitment to openness and that President Trump has delivered on the public’s demand for the truth. Earlier in the year President Trump directed the Pentagon and other agencies to identify and publicly release files on “unidentified anomalous phenomena” and potential nonhuman intelligence. The first wave of released material included grainy, black‑and‑white images that some observers say raise more questions than answers, while UAP advocates view it as a first step toward uncovering what the government may be hiding. Seth Shostak of the SETI Institute said the initial tranche of information reveals nothing of substance, and Richard Hoffman of the Scientific Coalition argued that the videos lack context and contain no scientifically valuable data.
summaries.answers-community-delivered-pentagon-wai: BBC lists a UAP-related report dated 2026-05-09 under the headline "The UFO community has been waiting for answers. Has the Pentagon delivered?". The headline is treated as the source claim, and the app does not add details that are not present in the feed metadata. The feed metadata connects the topic with PENTAGON. summaries.artemis-back-decades-deep-files-humanity: NASA has long projected that there is no confirmed evidence of alien spacecraft visiting Earth, a stance that remains unchanged in its official position. The Trump administration’s Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters (PURSUE) has been releasing previously hidden files that include military encounters, radar tracking incidents, astronaut observations, and classified investigations spanning decades. These newly released files show that numerous U.S. government agencies have treated the UFO phenomenon as serious enough to warrant classified investigations, intelligence monitoring, aerospace analysis, and national‑security scrutiny for decades. Historically, the public was led to believe UFO sightings were largely due to weather balloons, optical or atmospheric distortions, camera artifacts, or misidentifications, and NASA publicly explained many astronaut sightings as ice particles, reflected sunlight, orbital debris, or camera anomalies. The contrast now is that the federal system openly acknowledges unresolved encounters involving astronauts, military pilots, advanced radar systems, transmedium objects, and unidentified craft with capabilities not easily explained by conventional aerospace engineering. The contradiction highlighted by the article is not that NASA lied outright, but that its public tone of certainty and dismissal has shifted to a more cautious acknowledgment of unexplained phenomena.
summaries.anomalous-department-effort-files-histor: On May 8 2026 the Department of War announced the initial release of new, never‑before‑seen files on Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena (UAP) as part of the Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters (PURSUE). The interagency effort includes the White House, the Office of the Director of National Intelligence, the Department of Energy, the War’s All‑Domain Anomaly Resolution Office, NASA, the FBI, and other U.S. intelligence components. The collection will be housed on WAR.GOV/UFO and additional files will be released on a rolling basis. The release follows President Donald J. Trump’s direction to identify and declassify UAP files for total transparency, a level of disclosure that no other president or administration has achieved. Many of the materials have not yet been analyzed for resolution of anomalies, although all have been reviewed for security purposes. The FBI, NASA, and other officials have publicly affirmed the commitment to transparency while emphasizing that national security remains a priority.
summaries.analyzing-file-japan-near-pentagon-sight: Tokyo is analyzing a trove of previously classified Pentagon files on alleged UFO sightings that were released on Friday. The trove includes two videos of unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAP) spotted near Japan. Chief Cabinet Secretary Minoru Kihara said the government is constantly gathering and analyzing information on incidents related to national security, including UAPs. Kihara stated that Japan maintains close coordination with the United States and other countries on these matters. When asked whether Japan might disclose similar UAP files it holds, Kihara said the government would make case‑by‑case decisions after considering various factors. He added that one of the factors considered would be the risk of exposing Japan's intelligence‑gathering capabilities.
summaries.file-includes-japan-near-pentagon-sighti: The Japanese government’s top spokesman announced that Tokyo is studying a trove of previously classified Pentagon files on alleged UFO sightings. The files include two videos of unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAP) that were spotted near Japan. Chief Cabinet Secretary Minoru Kihara said at a regular news conference that Japan is constantly gathering and analyzing information on incidents related to national security, including UAPs. He added that Japan maintains close coordination with the United States and other countries on these matters. When asked whether Japan might disclose similar UAP files it holds, Kihara said the government would make specific, case‑by‑case decisions. He noted that decisions would be based on a comprehensive assessment of various factors, including the risk of exposing intelligence‑gathering capabilities.
summaries.bright-but-files-hot-interpretation-leav: The Pentagon released a new batch of declassified UFO files on Friday, including videos, images, and transcripts from the State Department, FBI, and NASA, after President Trump announced a major document dump on Truth Social. The files contain reports of unidentified anomalous phenomena such as a 1994 Tajikistan cable describing a brightly lit object making 90‑degree turns over Kazakhstan, a 2023 Aegean Sea report of a UAP turning at 80 mph, and a 2022 North American video of a football‑shaped object. Other documents include a 1969 Apollo 11 debrief where Buzz Aldrin noted a bright light that might have been a laser, a 2023 FBI interview of a drone pilot who saw a linear object with visible bands, and a 1972 Apollo 17 NASA photo of three dots in a triangular formation. The Pentagon states that many of the cases remain unresolved and that preliminary analysis suggests some objects could be physical, but no consensus exists on their nature. Experts such as former All‑Domain Anomaly Resolution Office director Sean Kirkpatrick warn that without analysis the release may fuel speculation, while congressional Republicans like Rep. Anna Paulina Luna and Rep. Tim Burchett have called for further transparency and additional video releases. The 2024 Pentagon report found no evidence that the U.S. government has recovered alien technology or confirmed alien life, and the release is part of an ongoing effort to declassify UAP material that began in 2022.
summaries.corbell-disclosure-dives-dog-fight-jerem: The trailer for “SLEEPING DOG” offers a first look at a documentary that follows investigative journalist and filmmaker Jeremy Corbell and his controversial rise in the UAP disclosure movement. Directed by Michael Lazovsky, the film has been described as a real‑life “Disclosure Day” and charts Corbell’s transition from mixed martial artist to a prominent figure pushing for transparency around unidentified aerial phenomena. It will open in select theaters on May 8, 2026, before becoming available on digital platforms on May 12. The documentary explores Corbell’s long partnership with investigative reporter George Knapp and their attempts to bring military‑filmed UAP footage and whistleblower testimony to the public eye. It promises unprecedented access and never‑before‑seen footage from Corbell’s earliest investigations, featuring figures such as astronaut Edgar Mitchell, John Lear, Bob Lazar, David Grusch, David Fravor, John B. Alexander and others involved in the UAP transparency movement. The film is produced by Jeremy Corbell, Jim Martin and Michael Lazovsky and aims to provide an intimate, high‑stakes look at one of the most polarizing subjects of the modern era.
summaries.ahead-appear-congress-documentary-offici: The New York Post reports that eight of the 46 UFO videos that Congress has requested will appear in an upcoming documentary. The videos are slated to be shown before their official release. The report was published on May 9, 2026. The documentary is expected to feature the videos ahead of the official release schedule. The article is part of a cluster of New York Post coverage on UAPs.
summaries.access-apollo-astronauts-declassified-fi: The Department of War, under President Donald Trump and with support from the Director of National Intelligence, released a set of UFO files to the public on Friday, citing a desire for “complete and maximum transparency.” Trump posted on Truth Social that previous administrations had not been open on the issue and that the release was part of his promise to share information on unidentified aerial phenomena. The Pentagon has published about 161 declassified files, including FBI memoranda, Air Force intelligence reports, military footage, infrared imagery, NASA transcripts, and a 1948 Air Force report on “flying saucers” near sensitive military sites in Europe. Apollo astronaut transcripts in the archive record observations of bright lights by Buzz Aldrin in 1969, particles and flashes by Alan Bean, and Jack Schmitt’s description of lights as “like the Fourth of July,” though some reports suggest the lights may have been reflections. Military sensors captured several UFO sightings, such as an eight‑pointed star object, a football‑shaped craft, a super‑hot orb that jammed sensors in Africa, a diamond‑shaped UAP traveling at roughly 499 mph in a 2024 video, and a luminous sphere moving between wind turbines. The Department of War has stated it will release additional declassified videos and high‑resolution photos in the coming weeks, but officials note that most cases remain unresolved due to insufficient data rather than confirmed extraterrestrial origin.
summaries.162-declassifies-evidence-files-judge-pu:
The Pentagon has released a first tranche of 162 declassified UFO files, dated from 1948 to 2026, including 120 PDFs, 28 videos, and 14 images from the Department of Defence, FBI, NASA, and State Department, to increase transparency on unidentified flying objects.  
The archive contains unusual sightings reported by astronauts and pilots but explicitly states that it contains no definitive proof of alien life.  
Among the documents are old State Department cables, FBI files, and NASA transcripts that discuss a suspected Nazi aircraft built in the Black Forest in 1944, described by Paul Peyerl as a disc‑shaped, 6.5‑metre, remotely controlled craft with rotating outer sections and jet engines, and include a photographic negative taken at 7,000 metres and a still image from a hangar.  
The FBI notes that its documents contain neither recommendations nor conclusions, making it impossible to assess whether the alleged Nazi UFO actually existed.  
A retro‑styled website hosts the files and highlights sightings such as a bright object making 90‑degree turns over Kazakhstan and a light observed by Buzz Aldrin during Apollo 11, yet it offers no concrete evidence of extraterrestrial technology.  
Congress members are calling for further disclosure, experts warn the released videos can be misinterpreted, and President Donald Trump has said the release allows the public to judge the evidence for themselves.
summaries.but-files-green-hints-lots-men-released: The Department of Defense began the initial release of government files on Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena (UAP) on Friday, fulfilling an order from President Trump to disclose documents related to aliens and UFOs. The collection is hosted on a government site that displays black‑and‑white photographs of anomalies, and it includes documents from the DoD, State Department, NASA, and FBI. One file contains eyewitness accounts from federal employees who observed orbs in the dusk sky on two separate days in the Western United States. The release also features images capturing anomalies from the Apollo 17 and Apollo 12 moon missions. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said the files have long fueled speculation and that it is time the American people see them. The DoD noted that all files have been reviewed for security, but many have not yet been analyzed to resolve the anomalies.

summaries.alien-eight-files-footage-frenzy-online-: The U.S. Department of War has released a black‑and‑white infrared video that shows an eight‑pointed object moving across the sky. The footage was reportedly captured in 2013 by a U.S. military sensor platform. It is nearly two minutes long. The clip has resurfaced through a growing archive of declassified UFO and UAP investigation files. It has gone viral online. The video has sparked intense discussion across social media, UFO communities, and conspiracy theory circles.
summaries.alien-newly-origins-questions-raises-rel: The US government released a declassified video on May 8, 2026, showing a star‑shaped object moving erratically over the Middle East in 2013, and it remains officially unexplained. The footage, catalogued as DOW‑UAP‑PR38 by the All‑domain Anomaly Resolution Office (AARO), was captured by an infrared sensor on a US military platform and shows an eight‑pointed star with alternating arm lengths that moves, leaves a heat trail, and exits the frame twice. AARO reports that the case has no radar data, no corroborating sensor information, and is unresolved. Technical analysts attribute the appearance to a military parachute illumination flare, whose burning heat causes thermal blooming that produces the starburst pattern seen in infrared footage. The May 8 release also included Apollo‑era files that were already publicly available, and historian Amy Shira Teitel noted that these documents have been in archives for decades. AARO has stated that none of the thousands of UAP cases it has reviewed have produced verifiable evidence of extraterrestrial life or technology, and the release represents a shift in government transparency rather than proof of alien life.

summaries.decades-inside-presidential-program-purs: On May 8 2026 the Trump Administration announced PURSUE, the Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters, which reorganizes how the U.S. government identifies, reviews, declassifies, and presents information about unidentified anomalous phenomena. The first tranche of PURSUE released 162 files on war.gov/ufo that are available without security clearance, marking the most extensive federal disclosure of UAP records since the 1947 Roswell incident. PURSUE was not created by Congress; it began on February 19 2026 when President Trump posted on Truth Social that he would direct the Secretary of Defense and other agencies to begin identifying and releasing files related to alien life, UAP, and UFO. The directive relied on the executive branch’s established authority over classified national‑security materials, and Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell said the department would cooperate with interagency partners to carry it out. Within days the rebranded Department of War, the former Department of Defense, became the coordinating lead for the program. The article examines the statutory basis for PURSUE, the agencies bound by its mandates, and the legal, constitutional, and oversight implications of the initiative.
summaries.big-festival-oregon-puts-small-town-visi: The 26th Annual McMenamins UFO Festival in McMinnville, Oregon, runs from May 14 to May 17 and is hosted by the McMenamins Hotel Oregon, making it the second‑largest UFO festival in the United States after Roswell. The festival began in 2000 to celebrate the 1950 UFO sighting by Paul and Evelyn Trent, whose photographs of a metallic object received national attention and remain among the most famous alleged flying‑saucer images. Organizers describe the event as a place where skeptics and believers come together, offering speakers, live music, a parade, vendor booths, costume contests, and a costume ball. The 2026 schedule includes a variety of speaker presentations, a podcast recording, a street fair, a UFO trivia session, and a “Landing Party” at the hotel tent. Tickets are required for the speaker presentations and can be purchased through ufofest.com, while most other events are free. The festival’s timing coincides with the release of previously unseen “UFO files” from the federal government, adding to the event’s appeal.

summaries.encounters-presidential-pursue-reporting: The Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters (PURSUE) was created in response to a directive from President Donald J. Trump to increase transparency about unidentified aerial phenomena (UAP) and related government records. The Department of War, with support from the Office of the Director of National Intelligence, is coordinating a nationwide effort to locate, review, declassify, and publicly release unresolved UAP‑related documents held by federal agencies. The initiative involves dozens of agencies and the examination of tens of millions of records, many of which exist only on paper and span several decades. The Department of War will publish new materials on a rolling basis, releasing tranches of declassified documents every few weeks as they are discovered. The released files are classified as unresolved cases, meaning the government cannot definitively determine the nature of the observed phenomena due to insufficient data. The Department of War also plans to continue separate reporting on resolved UAP cases as required by statute. Release 01 of the PURSUE archive was cleared for release on May 8, 2026, and includes a statement from Secretary of War Pete Hegseth affirming the administration’s commitment to unprecedented transparency.

en.title: Pentagon releases initial batch of declassified files detailing UFOs
en.summary: The Pentagon released a batch of “never‑before‑seen” declassified files on UFOs after President Donald Trump directed the agency to do so earlier in the year. The files contain investigations of reported sightings that span decades, including a large FBI file with hundreds of pages of eyewitness testimonies and public reports from 1947 to 1968 and more recent military memos describing a possible small UAP in Iraq in 2022 and multiple glares or lights of unknown origin observed in Syria in 2024.
